text: I feel frustrated and a bit anxious here. My view is obscured by something, perhaps my own hand, and the urgent voices suggest something is going wrong. The uncertainty of the situation, combined with the request for someone (Abel) to stop, makes me uneasy and disconnected from my surroundings. I long for clarity, for a moment of stillness where I can truly observe and understand what is happening. In this confusion, I am reminded of how fragile and contingent our perception of the world can be.
